title: Niphobolus lycopodioides

The Polypodiaceae family is a large and diverse group, with over 2,000 species of ferns worldwide. Niphobolus lycopodioides is native to tropical Africa, where it can be found growing in moist, shaded areas. This species is often classified as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows on land rather than in aquatic environments.
uses: Niphobolus lycopodioides, also known as the False Forked Fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
